papers.title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
Toolformer: Language Models Can Teach Themselves to Use Tools
Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior
Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
A Survey on Large Language Model Based Autonomous Agents
MetaGPT: Meta Programming for a Multi-Agent Collaborative Framework
CAMEL: Communicative Agents for Mind Exploration of LLM Society

chunks.text: ReAct prompts a language model to generate interleaved reasoning traces and task-specific actions: it reasons about a task, acts on an external environment such as a search API, and feeds the resulting observations back into further reasoning.

In contrast to chain-of-thought prompting, which only produces internal reasoning, ReAct grounds its reasoning in actions and observations, which reduces hallucination by letting the agent consult external sources.
Toolformer is trained in a self-supervised way to decide which APIs to call, when to call them, and how to fold the returned results back into its generation.
Reflexion turns feedback from a failed attempt into a verbal self-reflection that is stored in memory and used to improve the agent's next attempt.
Generative agents store a stream of experiences in a memory module and periodically synthesize higher-level reflections that inform planning of future behaviour.
Voyager incrementally builds a skill library of reusable, executable behaviours, enabling open-ended exploration and lifelong skill acquisition.
MetaGPT encodes standardized operating procedures into a multi-agent system so that specialized role agents collaborate and hand off intermediate artefacts.
Surveys of LLM-based autonomous agents organize the field around construction, planning, memory, tool use, and evaluation, providing a concept map of the area.

misconceptions.wrong_model: ReAct is just chain-of-thought prompting - the model only produces reasoning text.
misconceptions.correct_model: ReAct interleaves reasoning traces with actions and observations from an external environment.

quiz_items.question: In one sentence, what makes ReAct different from plain chain-of-thought prompting?
Besides reasoning, what does a ReAct agent produce and consume on each step?
What does Toolformer learn to decide?
What does Reflexion store after a failed attempt?
